def: s.m. Old age, senility; ancientness.

s.f. Increase, increment, augmentation; growth, rise; rising, ascending; — excess; a heap, quantity, assemblage, multitude; — increase in wealth or dignity, &c.; progression (of any kind); prosperity, success; advancement; happiness; — increase of the digits of the sun or moon; — wealth, property; — profit, gain; usury, interest (syn. sūd); — (in Med.) enlargement of the scrotum (either from swelled testicle or hydrocele or other morbid affections); — (in Gram.) a peculiar increase or lengthening to which vowels are subject under certain conditions (e.g. ā is the vr̤iddhi of a; ai of i, ī, and e; au of u, ū, and o); — (in Astron.) a particular period or division of time, the eleventh of the astronomical yogas (or the yoga star of the eleventh lunar mansion); — (fr. rt. vardh) cutting off, abscission; (in Law) forfeiture; deduction; — vr̤iddhi-śrāddh, s.m. A śrāddh or offering made to departed ancestors on any prosperous occasion (as on the birth of a son, &c.)
more: n. f. 1. Increase, especially the increase of money; interest. 2. ( Gram .) A peculiar protraction to which vowels are subject under certain conditions.
subs: or vr̤iddhi-mant (f. -matī), or vr̤iddhi-wān (f. -vatī), adj. Increased, augmented; increasing; prosperous; rich, wealthy
s.f. 'Wheel-interest,' interest upon interest, compound interest.

adj. = vr̤ihat, q.v.

adj. (f. -ī), Abounding with trees; consisting of trees

s.m. A scorpion; — the sign Scorpio (see biććhū); — a hairy caterpillar; — a crab; — a kind of beetle (found in cow-dung); — a centipede; — the thorny shrub Vangueria spinosa.

(˚sha+ut˚), s.m. Setting a bull at liberty on the occasion of a sacrifice or obsequial oblation, or as a religious act generally.

def: s.m. 'The rainer,' &c., an epithet of Indra

s.f. 'Wife of Vrishákapi'; epithet of the Dawn (as the wife of the Sun); — epithet of Lakshmī; of Gaurī; of Svāhā (wife of Agni); of Saćī (wife of Indra).

s.m. A woman of the Śūdra caste; — a woman during menstruation; — a barren woman; — the mother of a still-born child.
adj. A corr. of (or perhaps a mistake for) visham, q.v.
s.m. Name of a descendant of Yadu and ancestor of Krishṇ; — an epithet of Krishṇ, of Indra, of Agni
subs: s.m. (f. -inī), Name of a tribe or family, descendants of Vrishṇi and relations of Krishṇ; — name of a class of Kshatriyas and Vaiśyas.

def: s.m. See s.v. vr̤isha or vr̤ish.

(fr. rt. vr̤it, 'to turn,' &c.), Turned; rounded; round, circular; — been, existed, lived; happened, occurred; past, gone; finished; dead, deceased; — done, performed; acted; engaged in, undertaken; — (rt. vr̤it, 'to choose') chosen, selected, appointed; — (S. vr̤ittam, rt. vr̤it, 'to turn'), s.m. A circle; circumference; — event, occurrence; — procedure; practice; profession, occupation, mode of life, means of subsistence, livelihood; — act, action, behaviour, manner, demeanour; conduct; — observance of any enjoined practice, established rule or usage, actual practice, law; — rhythm; verse, metre

s.m. Any document containing a statement of events; a newspaper; a report (of)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Explaining (or explainer of) actions, or established practices, &c. (see vr̤itt)

s.f. A corr. form of vr̤itti, q.v.

s.f. Turning round; the circumference (of a circle or wheel); — being, existing, abiding, staying, remaining; existence; state, condition, proceeding, career, course of conduct, behaviour, action; operation; practice, business, profession, function, office, employment; mode of life; — livelihood, maintenance, means of subsistence; allowance, pension, &c. (see br̤itt or br̤it).
vodka

s.m. pl. (of waḥsh), Wild beasts.

ety: inf. n. of وجب 'to be necessary,' &c.

ety: inf. n. of وجد 'to be found; to exist,' &c.
def: s.m. Being found; invention; — being, existence; entity; life: essence, substance; — body; person, individual; — penis
more: n. m. وجد being. 1. Being. 2. Existence; entity; life. 3. Substance; body; material frame. 4. (Slang) Membrum virile. vujūd pānā, yā pakaṛnā , v. n. 1. To come into existence; come to be. 2. To be embodied, incarnated. vujūd adam-vajūd . Existence or non-existence. vujūd meṅ lānā . To give being; to create. bā-vujūd, bā vajūde ki . Notwithstanding; in spite of.
(from وجد) s. m. 1. Invention. 2. Existence, essence, being, substance, body. 3. Penis. باوجود bā-wujūd or باوجودي bā-wujūde, With the existence, notwithstanding.
subs: or wujūd pakaṛnā, To come into existence; to take form or shape; to become incarnate
(-ko), To bring into existence; to invent; to form, create
or bā-wujūde, Withal; notwithstanding (that, -ki); in despite (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وُجُود (wujūd), borrowed from Arabic وُجُود (wujūd).

def: ontology
s.f. pl. (of wajh, q.v.), Faces, aspects; — ways, modes; reasons, causes; — chiefs (of a country, city, or society).

def: s.m. pl. of wakīl, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of وقع 'to fall out, to happen,' &c.
def: s.m. Falling out, befalling, happening, coming to pass; — an occurrence, event; an accident, a contingency

To commence; begin to be; ensue. vuqū meṅ āyā . 1. Happened. 2. Committed; perpetrated. vuqū meṅ lānā , v. a. To cause; commit. vuqū h. , v. n. 1. To happen. 2. To be perpetrated.
(from وقع Heb. ) s. m. A contingency, accident, event, occurrence, the happening, the coming to pass.
subs: or wuqūʻ-ě-jurm-ke-waqt, adv. 'On the occurrence of the crime,' red-handed, flagrante delicto
To come to pass, to take place, to occur, happen
To bring to pass, to cause to happen, to commit
v.n. To happen, occur, take place; — to be committed or perpetrated.

(from وقف) s. m. Understanding, information, sense, experience, wisdom. وقوفدار wuḳūf-dār, Informed of, experienced.
subs: or wuqūf pakaṛnā, To get sense, &c.
adj. & s.m. Senseless, stupid, foolish; — a stupid fellow, a blockhead.

adv. = wahīṅ, q.v. adv. That very place; in that same or very place, just there; — that very time, that same or very instant, just then, precisely at that moment; immediately, at once; forthwith; — in that very manner, just so. even so.
more: adv. That very (time, place, &c.), immediately, exactly there, on the spot, in that very manner.

def: s.m. Appearing; descending, alighting; approaching, approach; coming, arriving, arrival
more: (from ورد Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Arrival, coming, appearing, approaching, descending, alighting.

ety: for A. وسعة, fr. وسع wusʻ, 'capacity,' &c., v.n. fr. وَسِعَ
def: s.f. Latitude; amplitude; spaciousness; capacity; space, extent; space covered, area; dimensions; bulk; — convenience, ease; opportunity, leisure
more: n. f. وسع extended. 1. Extent; space; area. 2. Latitude; amplitude. 3. Dimensions; capacity. vusat d., vasī k. , v. a. To extend; stretch; enlarge. vusat rakhnā , v. n. To occupy; contain; fall. vusat na rakhnā , v. n. To be unable to contain, pay, etc. māl-i-kasīr-ul-vusat , G. G. Goods of bulk.
(from وسع Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. 1. Latitude, amplitude, space, extent. 2. Leisure, convenience, opportunity, ease.
subs: (-ko), To give amplitude, &c. (to); to enlarge, extend, stretch, &c.
(-kī), To possess capacity, space, or room (for); to be able to contain, or to pay, &c.; — to occupy; to contain
adj. Of much or great capacity; very spacious; — of large size, of great bulk.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وُسعَت, from Arabic وُسْعَة (wusʕa).

def: s.m. = وصلت wuṣlat, q.v. s.f. Conjunction, joining, meeting, connexion; — a link or bond of union.

def: s.f. Conjunction, joining, meeting, connexion; — a link or bond of union.
more: s. f. Conjunction, joining, meeting, connexion.

s.m. Confiding(in), trusting (to); confidence, trust, reliance, dependence; — strength, firmness, stedfastness.
more: s. m. Strength, firmness, steadfastness, confiding in, trusting to.

Recovery of a debt. vusūl k. , v. a. To collect; realize; raise by assessment. vusūl karne-vālā . A receiver of collections. vusūl nīlām . Sale proceeds; monies realized by public sale or auction. vusūl huā . Realized; accrued.
(from وصل) s. m. Arrival, conjunction, acquisition or enjoyment (of any thing desired). وصول کرنا wuṣūl karnā, To collect. وصول هونا wuṣūl honā, To get, to be arrived.
subs: s.f. Arrear, uncollected balance; — realization or recovery of arrears or balances
v.t. To receive, get, collect, realize, recover; to raise (by assessment)
s.m. A receiver of collections, a collector, &c.
s.m. Money realized by public sale or auction, sale-proceeds
v.n. To be received, or collected, or realized, &c.; to accrue.

def: adj. That may be collected or realized, recoverable; — s.f. Recoverable dues.

adv. As many as that, so many.

def: s.m. pl. (of wazīr, q.v.), Viziers, privy-counsellors, ministers of state.

ety: for A. وضوء waẓūʼ, v.n. fr. وضأ
def: s.m. Sacred ablution performed before prayer, and which consists in washing, first the hands, then the mouth inside, then throwing water on the forehead, washing the whole face, the arms, and lastly the feet)
more: n. m. Ablution before prayer. It consists in washing the hands, then rinsing the mouth and cleaning the nose inside, next, throwing water on the forehead and washing the face, and lastly, the feet—all three times. vuzū tāzah k. , v. a. To perform ablution over again. vuzū toṛnā , v. a. To defile or destroy the purity or efficacy of one's devotions. Dekh lenā ki terā ham bhī vuzū toṛeṅge, Mohtasib! tū ne agar shīshah hamārā toṛā! I too impure will your ablutions make, Censor! if you my bottle dare to break! vuzū ṭūṭnā , v. n. To be destroyed (the efficacy of one's devotions). Uskī sūrat se zāhidoṅ ke vuzū ṭūṭte haiṅ .

prop. wuẓū (from وضأ or وضؤ) s. m. Ablution (performed before prayer; and, it consists in cleaning the teeth, washing first the hands, then the mouth inside, then throwing water on the forehead, washing the whole face, the arms, and lastly the feet).
subs: (-kā), To defile or destroy the purity or efficacy of the devotions (of)
(-kā), The purity or efficacy of (one's) devotions to be destroyed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وُضُو (wuzū), borrowed from Arabic وُضُوء (wuḍūʔ).
